assertions.title: ReportPage displays video players per scenario-persona

steps.action: Navigate to ReportPage for audit with video URLs
Check for 'Session Recordings' heading after 'Screenshot Timeline'
steps.expected: Session Recordings heading visible after Screenshot Timeline
steps.observed: H2 'Session Recordings' appears immediately after 'Screenshot Timeline' in heading order. Subtitle reads 'Browser session recordings captured during the audit journey.'

steps.action: Check for video cards with scenario/persona labels
steps.expected: 4 video cards with correct scenario-persona labels
steps.observed: 4 video cards rendered in a video-grid: Cookie Consent/Privacy Sensitive, Cookie Consent/Cost Sensitive, Checkout Flow/Privacy Sensitive, Checkout Flow/Cost Sensitive. Labels are correct.

steps.action: Check for HTML5 <video> elements with controls attribute
steps.observed: 0 <video> elements in DOM. Source code contains <video controls preload='metadata'> but the 37-byte mock placeholder .webm files trigger media errors, causing the onError handler to replace <video> elements with 'Video unavailable' error state.

steps.action: Verify video URLs serve content from backend
steps.expected: Video URLs return HTTP 200 with video/webm content-type
steps.observed: curl -I confirms HTTP 200, content-type: video/webm, content-length: 37 for all 4 URLs. Files are served correctly but are invalid/placeholder webm content that browsers cannot decode.

evidence.network: GET /api/audits/e2ed0938-ca82-4e84-b76d-ec788e860c51 -> 200 with video_urls dict; HEAD /artifacts/videos/.../cookie_consent_privacy_sensitive.webm -> 200 video/webm 37 bytes

assertions.issues: The <video> element code path exists in ReportPage.tsx (line 196) with controls and preload='metadata' attributes, but the mock placeholder .webm files (37 bytes) are not valid webm files, causing browser media errors. The onError handler replaces the <video> element with a 'Video unavailable' error message. The section structure (heading, grid, cards, labels) is fully correct. The failure is in the mock video data quality, not the UI component logic.

assertions.title: Video error state when URL fails to load

steps.action: Navigate to ReportPage for audit with video URLs where videos trigger errors
steps.expected: Video error state displayed for unreachable/invalid videos
steps.observed: All 4 video cards show error state with camera emoji (📹) and 'Video unavailable' text message
'Video unavailable' displayed with 📹 icon in a styled video-error container. No broken elements visible.
No console errors detected via agent-browser errors command

evidence.network: Video URLs return 200 but content is invalid webm, triggering HTML5 media error event

assertions.title: No video player rendered when video_url is null

steps.action: Navigate to ReportPage for old audit without video URLs
steps.expected: ReportPage loads without broken video elements
Section omitted or shows 'No recordings available'
steps.observed: Session Recordings section present with subtitle 'Browser session recordings are not available for this audit.' and empty state message 'No recordings available'. 0 video-error elements. Clean degradation.

assertions.title: PersonaDiffPage includes video access for each persona

steps.action: Navigate to PersonaDiffPage for audit with video URLs
steps.expected: PersonaDiffPage loads with video access per persona
steps.observed: PersonaDiffPage loaded at /audits/e2ed0938-ca82-4e84-b76d-ec788e860c51/diff (note: route is /diff not /persona-diff)

steps.action: Check each persona column for video player or Watch session link
steps.expected: Video players or Watch links per persona column
steps.observed: Each persona column (Privacy Sensitive, Cost Sensitive) has a 'SESSION RECORDINGS' heading (H4) with two '🎬 Watch' links. Privacy Sensitive has Watch #1 (cookie_consent_privacy_sensitive.webm) and Watch #2 (checkout_flow_privacy_sensitive.webm). Cost Sensitive has Watch #1 (cookie_consent_cost_sensitive.webm) and Watch #2 (checkout_flow_cost_sensitive.webm).

steps.action: Verify videos can play independently per persona
steps.observed: Each persona has independent links pointing to different video URLs. Links open videos in separate contexts allowing independent playback.

assertions.title: Multiple videos per audit don't block page rendering

steps.action: Check source code for lazy loading (preload='metadata' or equivalent)
steps.observed: ReportPage.tsx line 199 contains <video className='video-player' controls preload='metadata' onError={handleError}>. The preload='metadata' attribute is correctly set to prevent full video preloading.

steps.action: Navigate to ReportPage with 4 videos and measure page load time
steps.observed: Page loaded in 19.1ms (performance.timing). Page is fully interactive immediately. Document readyState=complete. No observable lag or freeze.

steps.action: Verify page renders content above and below video section
steps.expected: All sections rendered (Executive summary through Findings and evidence)
steps.observed: All 6 sections rendered: Executive summary, Persona comparison, Scenario breakdown, Screenshot Timeline, Session Recordings (4 video cards), Findings and evidence. Page is fully scrollable.

evidence.domInspection: Source code at ReportPage.tsx:199 confirms <video controls preload='metadata'>. Even though mock videos trigger error states (replacing <video> with error message), the preload='metadata' design prevents full video downloads. Page load time of 19ms with 4 video URLs confirms no blocking.

frictions.description: PersonaDiffPage URL in task prompt was /persona-diff but actual route is /diff. The route is defined as /audits/:auditId/diff in App.tsx.
frictions.resolution: Found correct route by grepping App.tsx and navigated to /audits/{id}/diff instead

frictions.description: All 4 mock placeholder .webm video files are only 37 bytes and are not valid webm content. Browsers cannot decode them, triggering HTML5 media error events. This causes all <video> elements to be replaced with 'Video unavailable' error state, making it impossible to verify actual video playback in mock mode.
frictions.resolution: This is a mock data quality issue. The placeholder files need to be valid minimal webm files (typically 200+ bytes minimum) for browsers to decode them.

summary: Tested 5 assertions: 4 passed (VAL-VIDEO-004, VAL-VIDEO-005, VAL-VIDEO-007, VAL-VIDEO-008), 1 failed (VAL-VIDEO-003: <video> elements exist in code with correct attributes but are replaced by error state because mock .webm placeholder files are invalid/too small for browsers to decode). The video replay UI structure is correctly implemented — Session Recordings section with labeled video cards, error handling, graceful degradation for null videos, PersonaDiffPage Watch links, and lazy loading via preload='metadata' are all working as designed.
